name: Florida Pharmacy Technician Registration
description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Florida - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Florida Board of Pharmacy
provider.parentOrganization: Florida Department of Health, Board of Medicine

address.description: Department of Health, Board of Pharmacy, 4052 Bald Cypress Way, Bin C04, Tallahassee, FL 32399-3254

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Florida pharmacy technician license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Florida Board of Pharmacy lists a total initial registered pharmacy technician fee of $105: $50 application, $50 licensure, and a $5 unlicensed activity fee. The biennial renewal fee is $55.
Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Florida have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Florida. To work in Florida, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Florida?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Florida Board of Pharmacy says to allow 30 days for initial review of a registered pharmacy technician application.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Florida while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contact the Florida Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Florida.
